text:
COMBINED HEMATOLOGIC MALIGNANCY / STEM CELL TRANSPLANT SURVIVORSHIP CLINIC
ATTENDING: Hematology-Oncology / BMT
VISIT TYPE: Follow-up — 12-Month Post-Allogeneic HSCT Survivorship Assessment

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Renata Vasquez-Okonkwo is a 49-year-old female with a history of ELN adverse-risk AML (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, NPM1 wild-type, trisomy 8, DNMT3A R882H) who presents for her 12-month post-transplant combined survivorship/leukemia follow-up. She is accompanied by her husband.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Vasquez-Okonkwo originally presented approximately 13 months ago with leukocytosis (WBC 87K), severe anemia, thrombocytopenia, gingival bleeding, and splenomegaly, and was diagnosed with AML — FAB M1, with a high allelic ratio FLT3-ITD (AR 0.91), NPM1 wild-type, complex co-mutation profile including DNMT3A R882H, TET2, WT1, NRAS, and trisomy 8. She received standard 7+3 induction (cytarabine/daunorubicin) with midostaurin per RATIFY and achieved a morphologic and MRD-negative complete remission after a single induction cycle. Given her adverse-risk disease and excellent performance status, she proceeded to myeloablative allo-HSCT from a 10/10 MUD using FluBu4 conditioning with tacrolimus/MTX GVHD prophylaxis. She engrafted promptly (neutrophil engraftment day +20, platelet engraftment day +24) and had full donor myeloid chimerism by day +30.

Her post-transplant course was notable for Grade II acute GVHD (skin and GI, Glucksberg criteria) responding well to methylprednisolone 1 mg/kg with steroid taper, and subsequently mild chronic GVHD affecting skin (xerosis), eyes (sicca), and mouth (mucosal erythema, reduced salivation). At 9 months, the cGVHD manifestations were improving; tacrolimus was tapered to 0.5 mg QD. She was off letermovir after day +100 with no CMV reactivation. She returned to part-time and then full-time teaching by 7 and 10 months, respectively.

Today she reports feeling overall quite well. Energy levels are back to near-baseline. She returned to full-time work as a high school biology teacher roughly 2 months ago and is managing without difficulty. She runs short distances again on weekends. She has no new complaints of fatigue beyond what she considers normal.

Skin: She notes her skin feels largely normal. The forearm tightness and xerosis that was present at month 4 has resolved. No new rashes.

Eyes: Dry eye symptoms substantially improved. She uses artificial tears once or twice a day now, mostly in the evenings when working at a computer. No significant photophobia or blurred vision.

Mouth: Oral dryness has resolved. No oral pain, no mucosal lesions.

GI: No diarrhea. Normal bowel pattern. Good appetite, eating full meals without restriction.

Neurologic: The bilateral toe tingling that had been attributed to tacrolimus has completely resolved since tacrolimus was stopped at approximately 11.5 months. No residual numbness or weakness.

Infectious: No fevers, no night sweats, no infections requiring hospitalization or antibiotics since last visit. She had a mild URI approximately 6 weeks ago, self-limited, managed conservatively.

Endocrine/metabolic: Continuing levothyroxine. No new symptoms of hypothyroidism — no cold intolerance, no weight gain, no fatigue beyond what she ascribes to her post-transplant recovery. BP has been well-controlled per home monitoring.

Bone health: She is aware of the osteopenia noted on DEXA and has been taking calcium carbonate 1000 mg daily and vitamin D3 2000 IU daily since the last visit. No fractures or bone pain.

Psychosocial: She reports some ongoing adjustment and intermittent anxiety about relapse, particularly with any new symptom. She finds talking with her husband and her support group (leukemia survivor network) helpful. She is not currently seeing a therapist but is open to a referral if needed.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive: Mild residual cancer-related anxiety; mild computer-related eye dryness (greatly improved). Negative: No fever, chills, night sweats. No weight loss. No new adenopathy. No chest pain, no dyspnea, no cough. No abdominal pain, nausea, vomiting, or diarrhea. No dysuria. No rashes or skin breakdown. No oral ulcers. No bleeding. No bruising. No focal neurologic symptoms. No arthralgias or myalgias of note. No edema.

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- AML, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN adverse-risk — diagnosed and treated as above
- Hypothyroidism — on levothyroxine, pre-existing
- Mild hypertension — well-controlled, pre-existing
- Mild chronic GVHD (skin, eyes, mouth) — resolving/resolved (see above)
- Grade II acute GVHD (skin/GI) — treated and resolved
- Post-transplant osteopenia — on supplementation

MEDICATIONS:
- Levothyroxine 75 mcg PO daily (hypothyroidism)
- Lisinopril 5 mg PO daily (hypertension)
- Acyclovir 400 mg PO twice daily (viral prophylaxis, per protocol through month 12 then reassess)
- TMP-SMX DS 1 tablet PO three times weekly (PCP prophylaxis)
- Cyclosporine ophthalmic emulsion 0.05% (Restasis) 1 drop each eye twice daily (cGVHD sicca — may wean given symptom resolution)
- Calcium carbonate 1000 mg PO daily
- Vitamin D3 2000 IU PO daily
- Tacrolimus: DISCONTINUED at 11.5 months (off immunosuppression)
- Methylprednisolone: DISCONTINUED (tapered off months ago)
- Artificial tears PRN

ALLERGIES: No known drug allergies.

SOCIAL HISTORY: Non-smoker. Occasional alcohol — 1-2 glasses of wine on weekends (resumed). Married, living at home with husband. Two adult children who are supportive. High school biology teacher, returned to full-time work. Avoids crowded public spaces when immune-compromised symptoms arise but otherwise leading normal life.

FAMILY HISTORY: No known family history of hematologic malignancy. Mother with breast cancer in her 60s (deceased). Father with type 2 diabetes.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
General: Well-appearing, in no acute distress. Well-nourished. Alert and oriented x3. Affect appropriate and pleasant.
Vitals: BP 118/72 mmHg, HR 72 bpm, Temp 36.8C, SpO2 98% on room air, Weight stable.
Skin: No rash. No erythema. No xerosis of forearms. No sclerodermatous changes. No petechiae or ecchymoses. Well-healed IV access sites bilaterally.
Head/Eyes: Sclerae non-icteric. Conjunctivae with no significant injection. No photophobia on exam.
Oropharynx: Oral mucosa pink, moist, intact. No ulcerations. No erythema. Adequate salivary pooling under tongue. No thrush.
Lymph nodes: No cervical, supraclavicular, axillary, or inguinal lymphadenopathy palpated.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs. No JVD.
Respiratory: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes or crackles.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender. No hepatomegaly on percussion. Spleen not palpable below left costal margin. No masses. Normal bowel sounds.
Extremities: No edema. No clubbing or cyanosis. Peripheral pulses 2+ throughout.
Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Strength 5/5 throughout. Sensation intact to light touch bilateral lower extremities including toes — no residual neuropathy. Gait normal.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (obtained today or within past 1 week):
CBC: WBC 6,200/uL, ANC 3,840/uL, Hgb 13.1 g/dL, MCV 91 fL, platelets 231,000/uL
Differential: unremarkable, no blasts or immature forms
Comprehensive metabolic panel: Na 139, K 4.1, Cr 0.82 mg/dL, BUN 12, total bili 0.6, AST 28, ALT 31, AlkPhos 68, albumin 4.2 — all within normal limits
TSH: 2.1 mIU/L (normal on current levothyroxine dose)
Magnesium: 1.9 mg/dL (low-normal; patient previously on tacrolimus)
Peripheral blood FLT3-ITD PCR (quantitative): NOT DETECTED (below assay sensitivity threshold)
Peripheral blood WT1 quantitative PCR: 9 copies/10^4 ABL1 copies (normal, <50)
STR chimerism (peripheral blood): overall 99.9% donor; CD3+ T-cell 99.2% donor; CD33+ myeloid 100% donor — full donor engraftment maintained
DNMT3A R882H allele-specific PCR (peripheral blood): not detected
CMV PCR: NOT DETECTED

Bone marrow biopsy performed today (day +365):
Morphology: Normocellular marrow (approximately 45-50% cellularity) with normal trilineage hematopoiesis. Megakaryocytes adequate in number and morphologically normal. Erythroid precursors with normal maturation. Myeloid series with normal progression. NO morphologic evidence of AML. Blasts estimated <2%.
Flow cytometry MRD (10-color, sensitivity 0.01%): NEGATIVE — no aberrant blast immunophenotype identified.
FLT3-ITD PCR (bone marrow aspirate): NOT DETECTED.
WT1 quantitative PCR (marrow): 9 copies/10^4 ABL1 — normal.
Chimerism by bone marrow aspirate: 100% donor myeloid, 99.2% donor T-cell.
Conclusion: Sustained MRD-negative complete remission with full donor chimerism at 12 months post-transplant.

DEXA scan (performed last month): T-score -1.4 lumbar spine, -1.2 femoral neck — consistent with osteopenia, not osteoporosis. No fragility fractures identified.

---

IMAGING:
Surveillance CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (reviewed from last week, formal read on file): No lymphadenopathy above or below the diaphragm. Normal liver size and parenchymal attenuation. No focal hepatic lesions. Spleen normal in size at 9.5 cm. No pulmonary nodules or infiltrates. No pleural or pericardial effusion. No ascites. No evidence of myeloid sarcoma or chloroma formation. Overall unremarkable post-transplant imaging.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

1. AML, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, ELN adverse-risk — MRD-NEGATIVE COMPLETE REMISSION at 12 months post-MUD allo-HSCT:
Renata is doing extraordinarily well. All molecular markers of her original AML clone are undetectable — FLT3-ITD PCR, WT1, and even DNMT3A R882H are all below the limit of detection. Full donor chimerism is maintained at 100% myeloid. She has crossed the 12-month post-transplant milestone, which is clinically meaningful given that FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio AML carries significant relapse risk in the first 1-2 years. I have reviewed today's bone marrow results with her and her husband in detail. This is an excellent result. We discussed that relapse risk, while not zero, decreases substantially as we move beyond the 2-year mark. Continued surveillance will be maintained:
   - Peripheral blood MRD testing (FLT3-ITD PCR, WT1, chimerism) every 3 months for the next year, then every 6 months thereafter if continued stability.
   - Annual bone marrow biopsy at 24 months post-transplant (day +730) or sooner if peripheral blood markers raise concern.
   - Surveillance CT imaging: plan for repeat in 6 months (18-month post-transplant scan), then annually thereafter if unremarkable.
   - No indication for maintenance targeted therapy at this time (midostaurin or gilteritinib maintenance post-transplant is not a standard recommendation in this patient's setting outside of a clinical trial, and she has sustained MRD negativity).

2. Chronic GVHD — RESOLVED:
NIH Global cGVHD Severity: none (resolved). Tacrolimus stopped at 11.5 months, no flare since. Skin, eyes, and mouth are all at baseline or normal. Plan: Discontinue Restasis — she can continue artificial tears PRN if she wishes. Continue to monitor at follow-up visits. No systemic immunosuppression required. Will reassess TMP-SMX at 14-15 months and plan discontinuation per institutional protocol if she remains off all immunosuppression and counts are stable.

3. Peripheral neuropathy — RESOLVED:
Completely resolved since tacrolimus discontinuation. No further intervention needed.

4. Infectious prophylaxis:
Acyclovir to continue through 12 months (essentially complete now — plan to discontinue at next visit at 14 months given she is off all immunosuppression and counts are normal). TMP-SMX three times weekly to continue through approximately 15-18 months post-transplant given prior steroid exposure, then reassess. CMV PCR undetectable, as expected given prior letermovir prophylaxis and no reactivation events to date.

5. Osteopenia:
On calcium and vitamin D as above. Repeat DEXA in 1-2 years. Discussed weight-bearing exercise (she has resumed walking/light running — excellent). No bisphosphonate indicated at this time given T-score above -2.5.

6. Hypothyroidism:
Stable on levothyroxine 75 mcg. TSH 2.1, within target. Continue current dose. Routine thyroid function monitoring annually.

7. Hypertension:
Well-controlled on lisinopril 5 mg. Continue. Routine BP monitoring.

8. Psychosocial:
Discussed ongoing survivorship anxiety — this is entirely understandable and common. We spent additional time reviewing the reassuring bone marrow and molecular results. I reinforced that she should reach out to our team for any concerning symptoms rather than wait for scheduled visits. Provided contact information for our cancer survivorship counseling service. Patient declines formal therapy at this time but will keep the referral in mind.

9. Survivorship / Long-term monitoring:
   - Cardiovascular risk: She received myeloablative busulfan-containing conditioning plus anthracycline (daunorubicin x3 doses during induction). Recommended continued cardiovascular monitoring, healthy lifestyle, BP management. Repeat echo not required routinely at this stage given no cardiac symptoms, but low threshold to reassess.
   - Secondary malignancy risk: Counseled on elevated risk given transplant and conditioning regimen. Annual skin exam (increased risk of skin cancers in setting of prior cGVHD and immunosuppression), routine age-appropriate cancer screening as applicable (e.g., cervical, colorectal — per primary care). Referred back to primary care physician for coordination of standard preventive care.
   - Thyroid surveillance: Annual TFTs given prior total-body immune conditioning and pre-existing hypothyroidism.
   - Fertility: Discussed — myeloablative conditioning has rendered her functionally menopausal; patient reports amenorrhea since transplant. Referred to gynecology for genitourinary syndrome of menopause evaluation and hormone replacement counseling if appropriate.

Return to clinic in 3 months for routine post-transplant follow-up and repeat peripheral blood MRD studies. Patient and husband with questions answered; understanding verbalized. Excellent visit overall.

— Attending Hematology-Oncology / BMT (co-signed)
